item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have my issues with the execution of this article, but I'm willing to upvote because hahahaha that's fucking awesome. I'm a sucker for this sort of intrigue-layered period tech story - I know that's a very niche genre, but there's a reason I love Halt and Catch Fire.

The description is suitably frission inducing on its own, and the idea of the electronics system fusing into someone's back makes zero sense but goddamn if it isn't cool. My biggest actual issue is the dialogue - mostly the doctor's, since the AIs is just computer shit. Like, in what world is the average reaction to someone chewing their cheek off "well then"? That's just the most salient example, the dialogue for the doctor reads distinctly loosely, less character and more archetype. I also thought the Maxwellist surgical strike team all saying "YOU ARE TO BE LIBERATED FROM ENTROPY" then passing out was ridiculous and impossible to take seriously — saying nothing at all is often infinitely more intriguing than Saturday morning cartoon villain dialogue.

But the rest of the stuff is very much in line with my tastes. Computers working with stuff humans can't quite comprehend — it almost reminded me of Age of Ultron, amusingly. Good stuff, just rather rough around the edges.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice job. Really good format and article theme. Keep up the good work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oooh yes it's finally up! This was a pleasure to read during the crit stage and it looks like the addendum you added since then only made this better, +1 for this great contest entry!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was hooked from start to finish. Don't get that often +1. Really excited to see how the rest of this storyline ends.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like how it starts, but can't help but feel it falter near the end. Addenda 1 - 3 do a good job of building tension, but then 4 and 5 are just repeating stuff I already know. It's built up to be this big reveal but ends up not revealing much at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There is, actually! It matches the translated text from an earlier version of the article, but it's changed since, so it doesn't correspond to the current text. With the exception of the bottom-right quadrant, the symbols are a personal stylization of Elian Script, a unique way of writing the English alphabet to appear alien and unusual. The bottom-right quadrant is a series of symbols i made up for numbers, as the translation originally had a numeric code in earlier versions of the draft.

If you go look it up, you might even be able to tell what the original text was. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I’m curious: is there an actual logic to the alien symbols or is it just random?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Clinical tone does not mean write obtusely. -1

item.title: That Alien Message
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to not_a_seagull, Nagiros, HarryBlank, weizhong, qntm, and anyone else who I'm forgetting for looking this over as a draft.

You get a cookie if you know where the title comes from :D

Thank you for reading!
